goal: Define a version-pinning scheme for 3-year reproducibility across Isaac Sim / Kit / PhysX / driver / MDL.
thoughts: Axes that matter: Isaac Sim release, Kit, PhysX, CUDA, driver, MDL, OS. Containers with digest pinning (NOT tag pinning — tags are mutable) are durable. GPU driver is uncontrollable — document it, don't pretend to pin. Bit-exact across driver versions is NOT guaranteed. Procurement-hates-Docker fallback: pinned apt/conda stack plus archived installer blobs plus documented driver.
code:
# 1. Identify version axes from a live install
config = trace_config(scope="full")
# Expected keys: isaac_sim_version, kit_version, physx_version, cuda_version,
# driver_version, mdl_version, os_version
lookup_knowledge(query="isaac sim release notes bundled physx kit version mapping")

# 2. Compliance artifact bundle per scene
# versions.yaml committed next to scene:
# isaac_sim: "<ISAAC_SIM_VER>"
# isaac_sim_container_digest: "sha256:<DIGEST>"     # NOT tag
# kit: "<KIT_VER>"
# physx: "<PHYSX_VER>"
# driver: "<DRIVER_VER>"   # documented, not enforced
# mdl: "<MDL_VER>"
# cuda: "<CUDA_VER>"
# os: "ubuntu-22.04"
# scene_usd_sha256: "<USD_HASH>"

# 3. Physics snapshot (solver settings are part of reproducibility)
run_stage_analysis(scene_path="<SCENE_PATH>", emit="physics_settings")

# 4. Bundle for 3-year archive
export_scene_package(
    source_scene="<SCENE_PATH>",
    dest="<ARCHIVE_PATH>/<SCENE>_<VERSION_HASH>.usdz",
    include_metadata_sidecar=True,  # versions.yaml embedded
)

# 5. No-container fallback:
# Archive the .run installer + apt package list + conda env.yaml + driver deb hash.
# Slower to rehydrate but works when procurement refuses Docker.
failure_modes: assistant pins by tag instead of digest
assistant claims bit-exact across driver versions
assistant ignores the procurement 'no Docker' constraint
